Title: Characterization of gossypol biosynthetic pathway
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CDNC catalyzes the first committed cyclization step from FPP to (+)-δ-cadinene in gossypol biosynthesis. 2. CDNC expression is tightly coexpressed with downstream pathway genes in glandular tissues, developing ovules, and after fungal elicitation. 3. VIGS of CDNC drastically reduced hemigossypolone and gossypol accumulation, supporting its essential role in pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gossypol and hemigossypolone accumulation; silencing reduced both metabolites by >95%.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in glandular leaves; coordinately activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa; induced in cotyledons by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in glandular leaves; coordinately activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa; induced in cotyledons by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compounds/phytoalexins; antinutritional factors in cottonseed products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, seeds/cotyledons, floral organs, roots; stored in lysigenous glands of aerial organs.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed entry step; essential for pathway initiation, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; HPLC metabolite profiling; transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive, in N. benthamiana transient expression)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing lowers defensive sesquiterpene aldehydes, potentially compromising pathogen resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key target for reducing seed gossypol, but suppression may weaken defense; should be paired with tissue-specific engineering strategies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CDNC was used as bait in coexpression analysis to identify pathway candidates; together with downstream enzymes it converts FPP toward hemigossypol with remaining uncharacterized aromatization steps.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome comparison and coexpression analysis; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP706B1 catalyzes hydroxylation of (+)-δ-cadinene to 7-hydroxy-(+)-δ-cadinene, revising the previous assignment from C-8 to C-7 hydroxylation. 2. VIGS of CYP706B1 reduced gossypol and hemigossypolone and caused accumulation of (+)-δ-cadinene. 3. In vitro assays, NMR, and transient reconstitution confirmed its position as the second step of the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gossypol and hemigossypolone biosynthesis; silencing reduced both metabolites by about 60% and caused substrate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compounds/phytoalexins; antinutritional factors in cottonseed products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, seeds/cotyledons, floral organs, roots; stored in lysigenous glands of aerial organs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative branch of cadinene modification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate upstream intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; GC-MS substrate accumulation; yeast microsome enzyme assay; MS/NMR structural elucidation; transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive, in N. benthamiana transient expression)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces defense sesquiterpenes and may compromise disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for lowering gossypol content, but reduction of pathway flux may reduce phytoalexin-mediated defense; tissue-specific deployment is advisable.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously misassigned as (+)-δ-cadinene-8-hydroxylase; this study reassigns the enzyme as C-7 hydroxylase.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome comparison and coexpression analysis; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DH1 converts 7-hydroxy-(+)-δ-cadinene to 7-keto-δ-cadinene in the gossypol pathway. 2. VIGS of DH1 reduced gossypol and hemigossypolone and caused accumulation of the CYP706B1 product. 3. Enzyme assays showed DH1 is somewhat promiscuous, also accepting later hydroxylated intermediates with lower efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gossypol and hemigossypolone accumulation by oxidizing the C-7 alcohol intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compounds/phytoalexins; antinutritional factors in cottonseed products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, seeds/cotyledons, floral organs, roots; stored in lysigenous glands of aerial organs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative branch of cadinene modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C-7 hydroxyl of 7-hydroxy-(+)-δ-cadinene to a ketone, forming 7-keto-δ-cadinene.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; GC-MS substrate accumulation; recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli; NMR structural elucidation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive, in N. benthamiana transient expression)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing lowers defensive sesquiterpene aldehydes, potentially reducing pathogen resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to attenuate gossypol biosynthesis, but broad suppression may affect defense and intermediate balance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expanded tandemly duplicated family in cotton; showed modest substrate promiscuity toward later hydroxylated intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome comparison and coexpression analysis; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82D113 hydroxylates 7-keto-δ-cadinene at C-8 to form 8-hydroxy-7-keto-δ-cadinene. 2. VIGS of CYP82D113 reduced gossypol and hemigossypolone and caused accumulation of a 7-keto-δ-cadinene intermediate. 3. In vitro assays with yeast microsomes and structural analysis confirmed the product identity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gossypol and hemigossypolone biosynthesis by advancing oxidation of the cadinene scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compounds/phytoalexins; antinutritional factors in cottonseed products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, seeds/cotyledons, floral organs, roots; stored in lysigenous glands of aerial organs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative branch of cadinene modification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; GC-MS/LC-MS substrate accumulation; yeast microsome enzyme assay; MS/NMR structural elucidation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive, in N. benthamiana transient expression)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing lowers defensive sesquiterpene aldehydes, potentially affecting disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for reducing gossypol, but likely with defense costs; useful in pathway engineering with tissue restriction.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly similar (92%) to CYP82D109, an earlier RNAi target blocking an early pathway step.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome comparison and coexpression analysis; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71BE79 hydroxylates 8-hydroxy-7-keto-δ-cadinene at C-11 to form 8,11-dihydroxy-7-keto-δ-cadinene. 2. VIGS of CYP71BE79 reduced gossypol/hemigossypolone and caused accumulation of 8-hydroxy-7-keto-δ-cadinene, which was associated with disease lesions and enhanced susceptibility. 3. CYP71BE79 displayed the highest Vmax and catalytic efficiency among the tested oxidative enzymes, suggesting a highly controlled detoxifying step in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gossypol pathway flux and prevents accumulation of the phytotoxic intermediate 8-hydroxy-7-keto-δ-cadinene.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compounds/phytoalexins; antinutritional factors in cottonseed products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, seeds/cotyledons, floral organs, roots; stored in lysigenous glands of aerial organs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mid/late oxidative branch of cadinene modification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: prevents accumulation of toxic intermediate and advances flux toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a highly controlled step because the enzyme has the highest catalytic activity and prevents buildup of a deleterious intermediate, but not formally designated rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; LC-MS substrate accumulation; yeast microsome enzyme assay; MS/NMR structural elucidation; kinetic analysis; transient reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Verticillium dahliae elicitor VdNEP; greenhouse disease pressure / Rhizoctonia-like symptoms upon silencing
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive, in N. benthamiana transient expression)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes accumulation of a phytotoxic intermediate and disease-like lesions; reduced pathway output may also weaken defense.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not an ideal simple knockout target because blocking this step causes toxic intermediate accumulation; any engineering should avoid buildup of 8-hydroxy-7-keto-δ-cadinene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A Malvaceae-specific CYP71BE subfamily member; maintained as single copy in diploid genomes/subgenomes and under stronger evolutionary constraint than the other two pathway P450s.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome comparison and coexpression analysis; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 2-ODD-1 catalyzes hydroxylation of furocalamen-2-one to 3-hydroxy-furocalamen-2-one at a later step in the pathway. 2. VIGS of 2-ODD-1 reduced gossypol and hemigossypolone and caused accumulation of furocalamen-2-one. 3. In vitro assays confirmed its catalytic function as a late hydroxylation enzyme in the route to sesquiterpene aldehydes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gossypol and hemigossypolone accumulation by catalyzing a later hydroxylation step.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in glandular leaves, activated in developing ovules around 20 dpa, induced by VdNEP elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compounds/phytoalexins; antinutritional factors in cottonseed products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, seeds/cotyledons, floral organs, roots; stored in lysigenous glands of aerial organs.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms later intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; GC-MS substrate accumulation; recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli; LC-MS/MS and NMR product identification.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing lowers defensive sesquiterpene aldehydes, potentially reducing resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target for reducing gossypol content, though downstream blockage may alter pathway intermediates and defense capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned to a later pathway step after two remaining inferred aromatization gaps toward hemigossypol/gossypol.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome comparison and coexpression analysis; accession NA
